headline.title: Anthropic’s Claude reports widespread outage
headline.summary: Anthropic's AI chatbot Claude experienced widespread service disruptions on Monday morning, with thousands of users reporting issues accessing the bot.

simpleSummary: Today's edition was assembled directly from RSS feeds without AI curation.

tools.title: OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon
tools.summary: By CEO Sam Altman’s own admission, OpenAI’s deal with the Department of Defense was “definitely rushed,” and “the optics don’t look good.”
tools.content: By CEO Sam Altman’s own admission, OpenAI’s deal with the Department of Defense was “definitely rushed,” and “the optics don’t look good.”

tools.title: How OpenAI caved to the Pentagon on AI surveillance
tools.summary: On Friday evening, amidst fallout from a standoff between the Department of Defense and Anthropic, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced that his own company had successfully negotiated new terms with the Pentagon. The US government had just moved to black
tools.content: On Friday evening, amidst fallout from a standoff between the Department of Defense and Anthropic, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced that his own company had successfully negotiated new terms with the Pentagon. The US government had just moved to blacklist Anthropic for standing firm on two red lines for military use: no mass surveillance of Americans and no lethal autonomous weapons (or AI systems with the power to kill targets without human oversight). tools.title: A robot arm with puppy dog eyes is just one of Lenovo’s new desktop AI concepts
tools.summary:
The AI Workmate Concept can move and rotate to accomplish various tasks, but can it ever truly know love? | Image: Lenovo	

Alongside a handful of new laptop concepts (and a range of real products too), Lenovo used MWC to announce a pair of AI-based
tools.content:
The AI Workmate Concept can move and rotate to accomplish various tasks, but can it ever truly know love? | Image: Lenovo	

Alongside a handful of new laptop concepts (and a range of real products too), Lenovo used MWC to announce a pair of AI-based productivity companion concepts. Both are standalone desk devices designed to boost productivity while providing office workers with a bit of artificial dystopic companionship.
Lenovo describes its AI Workmate Concept as an "always-on desk companion,

creative.title: Inside the Anthropic-Pentagon breakdown: mass surveillance, autonomous weapons, and a rival deal waiting in the wings
creative.summary: New reports from the New York Times and the Atlantic paint a detailed picture of the final hours of negotiations between Anthropic and the Pentagon. At the center: bulk data collection on American citizens, a rejected cloud workaround, and a parallel
creative.content:
New reports from the New York Times and the Atlantic paint a detailed picture of the final hours of negotiations between Anthropic and the Pentagon. At the center: bulk data collection on American citizens, a rejected cloud workaround, and a parallel OpenAI deal that was already in the works.
The article Inside the Anthropic-Pentagon breakdown: mass surveillance, autonomous weapons, and a rival deal waiting in the wings appeared first on The Decoder.

creative.title: Anthropic's new prompt forces ChatGPT to reveal everything it knows about you
creative.summary:
Anthropic is capitalizing on OpenAI's bad press with a new import function for Claude. A single prompt exports your saved context from ChatGPT or other chatbots, letting you transfer it straight to Claude's memory.
The article Anthropic's new prompt

research.title: HumanMCP: A Human-Like Query Dataset for Evaluating MCP Tool Retrieval Performance
research.summary:
arXiv:2602.23367v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers contain a collection of thousands of open-source standardized tools, linking LLMs to external systems; however, existing datasets and benchmarks lack realistic, hum
research.content:
arXiv:2602.23367v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers contain a collection of thousands of open-source standardized tools, linking LLMs to external systems; however, existing datasets and benchmarks lack realistic, human-like user queries, remaining a critical gap in evaluating the tool usage and ecosystems of MCP servers. Existing datasets often do contain tool descriptions but fail to represent how different users portray their requests, leading to poor generali

research.title: An Agentic LLM Framework for Adverse Media Screening in AML Compliance
research.summary:
arXiv:2602.23373v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Adverse media screening is a critical component of anti-money laundering (AML) and know-your-customer (KYC) compliance processes in financial institutions. Traditional approaches rely on keyword-based
research.content:
arXiv:2602.23373v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Adverse media screening is a critical component of anti-money laundering (AML) and know-your-customer (KYC) compliance processes in financial institutions. Traditional approaches rely on keyword-based searches that generate high false-positive rates or require extensive manual review. We present an agentic system that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to automate adverse media screening. Our system im

research.title: Causal Identification from Counterfactual Data: Completeness and Bounding Results
research.summary:
arXiv:2602.23541v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Previous work establishing completeness results for $\textit{counterfactual identification}$ has been circumscribed to the setting where the input data belongs to observational or interventional distri
research.content:
arXiv:2602.23541v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Previous work establishing completeness results for $\textit{counterfactual identification}$ has been circumscribed to the setting where the input data belongs to observational or interventional distributions (Layers 1 and 2 of Pearl's Causal Hierarchy), since it was generally presumed impossible to obtain data from counterfactual distributions, which belong to Layer 3. However, recent work (Raghavan & Bareinboim, 2025) has formally characterized

culture.title: ‘The digital colonization of flyover states’: how datacenters are tearing small-town America apart
culture.summary:
The rapid rollout of datacenters across the US is creating a divide between municipal governments and residents
Wilmington, Ohio, resident Quintin Koger Kidd was so concerned last June with his local public officials’ alleged misdoings – open meeting
culture.content:
The rapid rollout of datacenters across the US is creating a divide between municipal governments and residents
Wilmington, Ohio, resident Quintin Koger Kidd was so concerned last June with his local public officials’ alleged misdoings – open meeting violations and other discrepancies – that he filed a complaint in court to have the mayor and city council members removed from their posts.
When Koger Kidd later heard that the city supported plans by Amazon Web Services to build a $4bn datacenter

culture.title: I’m on the Meta Oversight Board. We need AI protections now | Suzanne Nossel
culture.summary:
AI is transforming our world. Accepting independent oversight is the least companies can do to protect our rights
The speed with which AI is transforming our lives is head-spinning. Unlike previous technological revolutions – radio, nuclear fission o
culture.content:
AI is transforming our world. Accepting independent oversight is the least companies can do to protect our rights
The speed with which AI is transforming our lives is head-spinning. Unlike previous technological revolutions – radio, nuclear fission or the internet – governments are not leading the way. We know that AI can be dangerous; chatbots advise teens on suicide and may soon be capable of instructing on how to create biological weapons. Yet there is no equivalent to the Federal Drug Admini

culture.content:
Tell us how you’ve been using AI since signing up to our newsletter course on how to make it work for you
Our six-week newsletter course AI for the People is helping Guardian readers use AI in smarter, more mindful ways – and now we want to hear from you.
Have you found a fun or practical way to use AI in your everyday life? Maybe it’s helped you organize something that always felt chaotic, learn something you’d been putting off, or solve a problem you didn’t expect it to.
